What I like about that game version is that it has raised squares on the board (in the form of a grid) that allows the tiles to be seated but not locked in place.

This [[ASIN:1223063151 Tile Lock Scrabble]] board is not much different with regard to the "locking" feature except that there are 4 raised nibs at the corners of each square into which the tiles get placed. The tiles DO NOT lock in place; they are just restricted from excessive movement. Obviously, my other board is a little over double this size. This board only measures 10 inches on all sides so it's closer to a travel or portable size. Besides the plastic board, a cloth bag holds all 100 tiles and four tile racks are included.

There are only two things I do not like about this particular version. The biggest disappointment is that the rubber feet are not at the edges of the board. This means when the game is being played, the board can be easily tilted if someone leans on any one corner because the feet only support the center of the board. The second thing is that the game is mislabelled because the tiles DO NOT LOCK in place but they stay seated well (or are prevented from excessive movement) as long as the board is not tipped. These were the only two items that kept me from loving this version and rating this five stars. Other than this, I really really like [[ASIN:1223063151 Tile Lock Scrabble]]. I also think its competitively priced at $24.99 but that is the absolute max that I would pay for this version.

title: Crochet Stitches VISUAL Encyclopedia
text:
1/1/18 UPDATE:
I'm feeling very different about this stitchionary than I did nine months ago. Now that I've leaped into couture crochet with both feet, I appreciate the sophisticated stitches here. If
you're a new crocheter and want something more comprehensive, this volume would be disappointing and maybe contain too much shorthand.
Add to that the large number of stitches being specialty techniques like Tunisian, Bruges Lace, cables, etc, and the potential is there for frustration at the small amount of space devoted to simpler basic stitches.

However, now that I've devoured my resources for basic and intermediate level stitches, I find myself using this Visual Encyclopedia much more often. I'm digging in deeper with crocheted cables, and Tunisian crochet, and both of these sections have lots of unique stitch patterns I haven't seen elsewhere.
I love Bathsheba's Knot, a variation of Solomon's Knot that is more stable and perfect for ribbon yarn cowls.

I still have several serious beefs with this book, one being the small photos. There's a lot of blank space on most pages, so I wish that the charts had been larger and clearer.

An index would be really helpful, but the table of contents at the beginning is comprehensive.

The quality of this volume is much better than average, especially at this modest price.
I still keep my old Harmony Guide to Crochet at hand, along with the Crocheter's Skill-Building Workshop by Dora Ohrenstein.
For a stitchionary of borders I strongly recommend the latest Edie Eckman Every Which Way Crochet Borders.

title: Celebrate Learning Motivators Motivational Stickers
text: These stickers are bright and cheerful. They are large and feature brief but appropriate messages. They remove easily and stick securely. A great way to brighten  child's day.

title: Trust Me: A Novel
text:
Mercer Hennessey is contacted by her former editor to write a book about a sensational local murder trial, in which Ashlyn Bryant has been accused of killing her two-year-old daughter. Mercer accepts because she could use they money and the case interests her. She also hopes working on the book and helping to spotlight Ashlyn's guilt might help her get over her grief for her husband and young daughter who were killed in a car accident a little over a year ago. Mercer's publisher warns her than anything but a guilty verdict for Ashlyn could kill the book deal, but when the verdict comes in, Mercer couldn't never have guessed what would happen next.

I enjoy this author's series books, I was excited to read this new standalone novel. The story takes a lot of twists and turns, so much that things begin to get a little convoluted toward the end, but ultimately the last few twists are surprising and satisfying. Both of the main characters, Mercer and Ashlyn, are very complicated characters. Mercer is sympathetic and fragile as she deals with her grief, but smart and determined when investigating the murder of little Tasha Nicole. Ashlyn is clever and often manipulative, but is she a victim of a rough childhood, simply delusional, or is she evil? The murder of Tasha Nicole has many similarities to the real life death of Caylee Anthony and that case is referenced in the story.

Mercer doesn't know who to trust and the reader doesn't either, which keeps you guessing as to what is going to happen next. I was thinking the worst at the end, but after all the tragedy and heartbreak throughout the book, it ends on a hopeful note. Hank Phillippi Ryan is an excellent writer and the story she crafts in "Trust Me" will keep you guessing as to which character you can trust and what the truth really is until the very end.

title: Crusoe the Celebrity Dachshund 2022 Wall Calendar
text: Crusoe has had more than his share of health crises, so understandably, his pictures aren't as goofy and flamboyant as they were a few calendars ago.  Strangely, his humans haven't done more to have his little sister, Daphne, with her ditzy blonde cuteness and boundless energy, pick up the slack.  How about having Crusoe play his famous red violin while Daphne "dances" or "sings" along? It's still a funny calendar, but like last year's, it sort of falls flat.  I know Crusoe, Oakley, and Daphne are capable of SO MUCH more!

title: An ABC of Equality (Volume 1) (Empowering Alphabets, 1)
text: We love this book. Great information and entertaining to read.  my son wants to read it every night before bed.

title: Tile Lock Scrabble,2 to 4 players
text: For some reason, I was thinking I was going to get a full size Scrabble board with wood tiles and they just wouldn't slide around. Not so much. This is roughly three quarter size, and it works for what it's supposed to. But it's worth about 11 bucks.

title: First 100 Stickers: Words: Over 500 Stickers
text: Great to do with my daughter.  Only negative is that the sticker pages should be removable or located immediately after the page they go on vs all in the bk.

title: Settling the Account (Promises to Keep Book 3)
text: I enjoyed this third book in the trilogy because it finally had a happy ending.  I didn't see it coming, and could not be happier with the way it ended. Did have hopes that Amy would react at some point to the mean husband, though.  I will probably get the next book - Second Chances.

title: The New Quantum Universe (Revised and Updated Edition)
text:
When I bought this book, I thought this book is devoted to theoretical aspects of quantum mechanics, but actually it is focused mainly on its applications in technology. The authors discuss the basic concepts of quantum physics briefly; its history and incompatibility with classical physics and then discuss various applications.

Quantum mechanics teaches us as how atoms and subatomic particles behave and explain the fundamental process involved in making elements and stars. The classical physics explains the physical reality of larger forms of matter that includes molecules, life forms, planets, stars and galaxies. But unlike classical physics, the physical reality of quantum world is not easily comprehended. The wave-particle duality of matter brings forth strangeness of quantum physics that includes Heisenberg uncertainty principle, quantized states of matter, quantum entanglement, quantum tunneling, quantum jumps, etc.

The authors have avoided the mathematical and philosophical issues behind the theory and focused on its application that paved the way for opportunities and advancement in technology. Manipulating the matter at the quantum level has lead to the nanotechnology and quantum computing. This has lead to many exciting possibilities in computer engineering and information processing. Instead of bits of information stored in "1" or "0" in the present day classical computers; quantum teleportation allow the algorithms using quantum bits called qubits that simultaneously contain "1" and "0" by quantum entanglement. Quantum teleportation is a process in which quantum information is destroyed so that it may be transferred simultaneously to another location. This has been proposed as a way to create quantum communication networks and quantum computing protocols. Quantum information cannot be copied, but quantum cryptography has a high degree of security.

Quantum tunneling has offered tremendous opportunities in fiber optics, invention of scanning electron microscope, and nuclear fission reactions and atomic weapons. Semiconductors and superconductivities of some of the elements at low temperatures have lead to the discovery of superconductors and strange properties of matter that are used in technology. If you are interested in knowing what quantum mechanics can do to shape the future technology, then this book is very helpful.

title: The Glass Menagerie
text: I bought this for my son, as it was assigned summer reading before his senior year of high school.  This is written in play format (which I really don't enjoy), but it's a good story of an ordinary family in St. Louis; the daughter has a slight deformity, and her brother is assigned to find a "gentleman" to call upon her from his acquaintances at the shoe factory where he works.  Well written family drama.

title: The Dark Angel (Ruth Galloway Mysteries, 10)
text:
This is the 10th in the Ruth Galloway mystery series. There is a very strong over all story arc to this series so definitely begin reading with the earliest book available. This is the first book in the series that I have read which diminished my enjoyment greatly, something that I took into account when I reviewed the book.

Archeologist Ruth Galloway has been contacted by a colleague to consult on a discovery in Italy. The project, which included the opportunity to bring her daughter along, came at an opportune time....her married lover's wife had recently revealed that she is pregnant. The already stressful love triangle turned into a rectangle when Ruth found out that the baby might not be her lover's. Unfortunately the idyllic break in Italy turned out to be much more complicated than Ruth had anticipated.

This seems like an interesting series, at least enough so that I intend to seek out the earlier books to get all the back stories. The mystery element to this is definitely secondary to the rather convoluted personal lives of a rather large cast of charters.

title: Core HTML5 Canvas: Graphics, Animation, and Game Development
text: If you're an serious HTML developer and want to start using HTML5, this book is one of the best choices. The text covers a lot of material without a lot the tons of trivial examples that beginner-oriented books usually have. This book may be too intense for beginners or casual HTML writers, though.

title: Unleashing Mr. Darcy
text:
In many ways, this is one of my favourite romances I have read this year. I was actually quite surprised by how much enjoyment I got out of this book. What the characters were based on put aside, I loved the originality of the cast. When you read a lot of romances, they all tend to blend together after a while, so it was nice to see some characters that really made me want to root for their success.

I really loved Elizabeth's character. She was intelligent, emotional and just all around the kind of character that you want to learn more about. I understood why she felt the way she did and respected her for being different than the average female character who meets the rich, handsome bachelor.

Donovan was well written and the dialogue he brought to the story was better than the average romance lead character drama. I laughed out loud in multiple places at his observations of the other characters and the situations he found himself in.

The dog shows were well described and I loved that Elizabeth had such a soft spot in her heart for Bliss and for Sue's companions.

Romance-wise, this story was paced well and the characters didn't fall in love and end up in bed together within the first chapter. The author allowed them time to build a relationship before she embarked on much intimacy and this made me feel closer to them. I liked that we got to know more about them as individuals before they became a single unit. The sex scene itself was also tasteful and more romantic than gross for those of you who don't like really explicit descriptions.

I will say, that as much as it made me giggle at times, if you are English, the MANY references to what English people and English men in general are like will either make you laugh or drive you mad. In some places the cliches were so abundant it nearly reminded me of one of those "what American's think we do" memes you find on the internet.

There is a bit of imagination stretching to get around things like passports, visas and the ability to simply decide to stay in a country other than your own, but the story warranted a bit of belief suspension and that was okay. I thought the characters were worthy of it.

That and the repeated use of the phrase "Storm clouds gathered in his eyes" were the reason I gave this four stars rather than five. Still, this was a really fun way to pass the hours and I got a lot of enjoyment out of it, more so even than I had expected.

If you are a fan of the lighter romance with a good back story and lots of reasons to smile, this book will be just your thing.

This review is based on a digital ARC from the publisher and provided through Netgalley.

title: Dynamic Still Life for Artists: A Modern Guide to Essential Concepts and Techniques (Volume 7) (For Artists, 7)
text: I have been painting for many years but have not done still life so was happy to have this book to study.  It is fully illustrated in color throughout and discusses a lot of painting concepts and methodologies for still life, giving the user suggestions and ideas to keep in mind.  It's not quite the instructional or tutorial book I was looking for, but offers much inspiration to help me get set up and determine what to paint and how to paint it.

title: One to Watch: A Novel
text:
Bea is a size 20 in a size 2 world - has been all her life - enduring the ignorant slings and arrows of an unkind group of people wherever she goes. She’s got lots of other “plusses” than her weight - a great, loving family and status in the world of fashion bloggers, where she commands an army of thousands. The only thing missing is the right guy - one who sees “her” and loves her as she is.

So, when the offer came from TV’s “Main Squeeze” (a Bachelorette clone), Bea sees an opportunity to show the world that the overly endowed girl can be loved and appreciated - and she might end up with the man of her dreams in the process. All she asks is that they choose 25 men with open minds, few biases - and a range of diversity.

Here’s where I think the reader will learn a little about The Bachelor’s inner workings. Yes, Bea has a great wardrobe she gets to keep, and all she has to do is sift through the one-on-one dates and overnights to find Mr. Right. And, the staged experiences begin (TV has to have drama). She eliminates them by “kissing them off”.

Typical of The Bachelor, there are the real stinkers, the ones who pretend to care, the ones who take one look and leave (because they weren’t told ahead of time that they had a plus-size bachelorette), the ones who are just plain mean and so on.

How does this end? Does Bea see it through to the end? Does she find love? This novel is well-written - it’s a comfortable, sometimes page-turning read. And for fans of The Bachelor, illuminating!

title: Whoopie Pies : Dozens of Mix 'em, Match 'em, Eat 'em Up Recipes
text:
Where I live, we're all about the gobs (what others call whoopie pies). They're in the stores, in the bakeries, sold at the street booths in the foodie sections of town. So I figured that it was time to start making my own instead of spending money on them, and this book had come recommended. All in all, it's a great book - I love that there are so many different flavors in here: chocolate, lemon, pumpkin, even some savory ones. There's also a couple of vegan and gluten-free versions of chocolate and vanilla gobs which is great, as now I've something to make for a few friends when they visit (or to give out as gifts). There's also a plethora of icing flavors, and the book lets you mix and match (so you can make an almost endless amount of gob/whoopie pie combinations).

I've tried two gobs so far, the pumpkin and the 'classic' chocolate, and they have turned out tasting wonderful - people have raved over them. I love buttercream filling, so I went with the 'classic' buttercream and the matcha buttercream, and again, simply delicious. The instructions are easy to follow, the ingredients listed out, so a beginner shouldn't have a problem making these.

Thing is, I'm not a beginner baker - I've made cookies as easy as chocolate chips and as complex as lady locks/lady fingers. By no means am I a pastry chef, and I don't have a cooking school arsenal in my kitchen, but I'm pretty capable (or so I like to think) when it comes to baking. That said, I really, *really* wish this book had included some pictures more than the pretty ones showing the finished product - because it would help to know just how much of the batter to put on the cookie sheet, and what it was supposed to look like (because let me tell you, my pumpkin gobs did NOT look like the finished product in the book - they came out a bit lumpy and spikey). The authors had a helpful section at the front suggesting you use a melon ball scoop for the smaller gobs (it didn't work very well, I had way more cakes than they told me I would - is there that much size variation on melon ballers or something?) and 2 tablespoons/a small icecream scoops for the larger cakes (I couldn't find a 2 tablespoon anywhere so I used a 1 tablespoon twice, and I ended up w/ less cakes then they said I would). It's annoying to have to buy gadgets to figure out how to get the right 'size' for these cakes.

I also had to double the recipe of the matcha buttercream to have enough to fill the chocolate gobs. Granted, I had some extra because of the whole sizing debacle, but still, I wasn't that short of the icing - it wasn't just a few gobs left over, it was about half of the batch. In the future, I'll be sure to have extra icing ingredients on hand 'just in case', especially if the icing calls for half a stick of butter (the classic buttercream called for a whole stick and came w/ enough for the pumpkin gobs).

So all in all, the recipes that I've tried are delicious. The instructions are, for the most part, easy to follow and I haven't seen anything too exotic in this book by way of ingredients (when you consider the flavors). There is a lot of variety and you can have a good bit of fun mixing and matching flavors, so you shouldn't get bored for a while. I deducted a star because I wish there were clearer instructions or PICTURES on how to size the batter on the sheets, which is obviously going to take some trial and error to figure out.

title: Any Witch Way You Can (Wicked Witches of the Midwest Book 1)
text: This was a fun read. It was just the right kind of read for my bus commute. Not too demanding with likable characters and a fast paced plot.

title: Lonely Planet Pocket Cancun & the Riviera Maya 1 (Pocket Guide)
text:
The Lonely Planet pocket guides are an excellent series of little guides for locations all over the place. They are most convenient when planning a trip to another place and/or when you are in another place. This particular one would come in handy for Cancun & The Riviera Maya.
This guide contains everything from places to stay, sights to see, restaurants, nightlife, places to shop, hidden (or not so hidden now) things/places that may be of interest, and so much more. The guidebook contains colored maps all throughout for different locations (over 19), and a really cool full color pull out regional map in the back of the book. The full color pictures throughout the book are beautiful.
This is a great guidebook and a great series of books to check out if you are planning a vacation! These books are terrific and a great way to plan for a wonderful trip.

title: Forgiving What You Can't Forget: Discover How to Move On, Make Peace with Painful Memories, and Create a Life That’s Beautiful Again
text: I enjoyed the book. No a-ha moments but helpful. Spiritual. Only negative is the the print is very light making it hard to read.

title: The Cinder Spires: the Aeronaut's Windlass
text: Not my favorite work by Butcher, but it is very much in his style of story telling. I think my one complaint with it is I wish it was written at a higher reading level. I feel like it's written more for kids/teens then for adults. The language can be simple. I love the setting though, very cool.

title: Miss Knight and the Ghosts of Tsavo (Society For Paranormals Book 1)
text:
Bee is a young Victorian woman with the ability to see and discern ghosts, werewolves and other paranormal creatures. Ward of a wealthy British family, she has no options but to accompany them to Africa when their fortune takes a decidedly downward turn. There she confronts strange and dangerous happenings that equal or surpass what she encountered in England. Most pressing is the case of ghost lions that are terrorizing the area. Bee must juggle Victorian sensibilities while solving mysteries that could kill her.

This is a fun book. Bee is intelligent and witty. She ably coveys the farce of the privileged British attempting to impose their mores and lifestyle in a hostile environment populated with bemused natives. She also underscores the limitations imposed on women of that time. I wasn't crazy about the rather abrupt cliffhanger ending but overall this was a fun take on paranormal mysteries.

title: The Ghosts of Idlewood
text: tell their stories in many ways. In this case, ghosts wander and call to those who can help them rest easy. Smooth story line, well fleshed characters, and an easy segue to the next volume.

title: Greek Myths: Meet the heroes, gods, and monsters of ancient Greece (Ancient Myths)
text:
This is such a great book for young readers. It's a very nice introduction to the major heroes, gods, and monsters of ancient Greece. The information within is very well written and to the point but with that fun edge to it that promises to draw in even the pickiest of readers. The artwork is amazing. It has that ancient look to it but with vibrant colors that pull your eyes to the book and pages instantly.
What I'm trying to say is that this book is excellent. If you are a fan of mythology yourself you may like to add it to your own collection for the pure enjoyment of it. If you have youngsters I definitely recommend this for them as an introduction or to further their interest. It's a great book in all regards.

title: Fashion Design Studio: Learn to Draw Figures, Fashion, Hairstyles & More (Creative Girls Draw)
text:
I know Chris Hart from his other books and didn't realize he did "Fashion Design Studio" when I selected it so when I saw his name, I was a little 'Oh, let's see what he's got here.'

I was actually surprised in how much I enjoyed it. I'm not an artist but even I could follow the instructions. I did like the time spent at beginning with the "Getting Started". Even if one isn't going to go hardcore into fashion design, it was still a nice intro.

For a slim book, Hart covers a lot of ground. From the basics to the different angles to hair and shoes, etc., a lot is covered. Perhaps not enough in depth for someone who is serious about fashion design, but certainly more than enough for the budding creative artist which is who is the target audience.

It was easy and simple but also fun.

title: The Innocent (Will Robie Book 1)
text: Good prose with an intriguing plot and lots of twists and turns. Baldacci does a better than average job of creating realistic characters with individual voices. The plot is a bit far-fetched, but - hey! - we're looking for entertainment, right?

title: Go High: The Unstoppable Presence and Poise of Michelle Obama
text: Terrific coffee table book. 50 full-color photographs with quotations by the former First Lady. I've never been in the cult of the First Ladys but she was admirable and these are a fun remembrance, and she is a person you can point to with integrity, perseverance and good morals.

title: How to Draw 101 Animals (1)
text: Probably the weakest of the series.  I picked up three others for my son for Christmas.  For the price, this is a great collection, BUT it isn't nearly as exciting as the 101 Dinosaurs or 101 Monsters.  He's still going to enjoy it, but I think the others will probably be better liked.  The drawings are easy enough and easy to follow.  I know I always had fun with this type of drawing book as a kid!

title: No-Waste Composting: Small-Space Waste Recycling, Indoors and Out. Plus, 10 projects to repurpose household items into compost-making machines (No-Waste Gardening)
text: i just wanted to learn more about composting and although the book starts with working in a large garden, later chapters go into other containers to achieve the same results and also a chapter for indoor composting.  Regardless of your space: so much knowledge here that I learnt to adapt within my space - and feel happy that no food parts are goin to waste and I can get some well nutrition soil for the next year without buying all the chemicals to do the same job.

title: Tell No One: A Novel
text: You think you have it figured out then another twist and again you think you have it figured out. But you don't. Not even as the story comes to an end. You won't see it coming.

title: Frankenstein
text: Well-crafted story with a simple moral: Don't reanimate corpses

title: The Rosie Project: A Novel
text: Some of my friends started a book club, and we read this one month. Well-written, very clever, and sensitive. Not hard to develop an emotional attachment to the characters, the story is compelling enough, and I found myself laughing out loud quite frequently. Probably wouldn't have read this one on my own, but it makes for a fun discussion with friends, and I really did enjoy it.

title: Shatter the Sky (The Shatter the Sky Duology)
text:
I'm not one of those people where the relationship, regardless of what it is, draws me to a book so that wasn't necessarily an initial draw. What hooked me was the dragon stealing. However, once you get reading the story, the relationship in the very beginning of the book between Maren and Kaia is adorable so when Kaia is taken away and Maren decides that she is going to rescue her by stealing one of the emperor’s coveted dragons and storm the Aurati stronghold. This is one heck of a task she sets for herself and when she finds herself learning secrets and rumors concerning rebellions, lost princes and more her life gets really complicated.
I enjoyed this book a lot. It reads quickly and keeps your interest. It's a great debut story, one that can only go up from here. I am looking forward to the next book in the series. This one leaves you stuck on a cliffhanger at the end which is incredibly frustrating! I'm terrible at waiting but I will do so impatiently! I recommend this book.

title: House Rules: A Novel
text: I really liked this book and so did my 24 year daughter.  We eagerly await each book by this author, they are always thought provoking.  This is a story about a near adult child with asbergers autism.  It tells of how difficult it is to grow up being different, and how easily people can be mis-understood.  It is a story of a mother's love and the reaction of a sibling when one child's needs take over the family routine and lifestyle.  I would recomeend this book to everyone.

title: Later
text:
This was. . . a short Stephen King book/novel. How utterly odd. It's a horror story. The narrator and protagonist make sure to let us less astute readers know that fact repeatedly. Jamie, Champ if you want to annoy him, sees dead people. He talks to them. The dead can't lie. Why is this? It's never established. Jamie's mom has and had depending on where you are in the story, a girlfriend who was or is, again, location location, location, a cop. Not a good cop. A naughty cop. This girlfriend, Liz, trips to Jamie's secret ability and to save her career she forces him to use his talent. The dead can't lie. . . but what about the things from outside that can ride the evil dead (man those were good movies), what about them? Well, you'll have to get a copy of 'Later' and read it now won't you? I'm certainly not giving away any more of the story.
In all honesty it had some spooky moments and some 80/90s gangster vibe. I've always liked the people and places King creates more than the "horror" aspect of the story (although I was standing on a raft in an oxbow of a river in a state where King had lived when I realized I was very likely on THE raft from his story. Now THAT was a spine tingling moment) anyhow, I like the people better than the jump scares. The book is well worth a read. Go read it. And leave a light on. . . if you can!

title: Everyday Vegetarian: A Delicious Guide for Creating More Than 150 Meatless Dishes
text:
This is a good, basic cookbook for Vegetarians with clear instructions from Cooking Light Magazine. The Vegan recipes are marked as such for ease of use. Each recipe has lovely photographs making each dish more tempting than the next. I ordered this so I could learn how to add extra protein in my diet without meat. This cookbook was a good start, but I was disappointed that there were no breakfast recipes. I would like to add protein at breakfast without using eggs, this cookbook did not offer any new ideas.
The first recipe I tried was a rice pilaf that was awesome and will be a good go to recipe for me. All the recipe so far have been delish and have widen my recipe collection for healthy meals.
Highly recommend!

title: The Song of Achilles: A Novel
text:
With so many reviews I'll keep mine short so you can keep reading them.
I was curious about how this book was going to be. Mythology is fun because it can be changed and rearranged as one sees fit as long as certain little details stay the same (hey, it is myth after all!). I love mythology and enjoy the different ways that people play with it.
This was very good. I liked most of it. The build up to the Trojan war was great and getting to see all the figures from all the stories throughout was so much fun. At the heart of the story though is a love story between Achilles and Patroclus. It was a sweet story for the most part. It went from almost a puppy dog love as Patroclus, like so many other men and women, was attracted to Achilles as soon as he looked at him. As time moves on they become closer. I really don't want to spoil this book for you so I'll stop there with that.
I really enjoyed most of the story right up until the end. I can't go into detail here but I will say right up until the first Hector part on. I can't really even give you my reasons for it. I suppose I just liked other versions of what happened here and couldn't quite get on board with this ending.
Anyway, the book is good and the writing is exceptional. I enjoyed the author's way with words and the smoothness of her writing. I'll likely move on and read Circe next. I'm curious to see where that one goes!

title: Invisible City: A Novel (Rebekah Roberts Novels)
text:
Rebekah Roberts has never known her mother. Rebekah was a baby when her mother left her boyfriend and baby to return to her Brooklyn Hasidic Jewish community. Rebekah is now living in New York City trying to make it as a reporter and a murder case brings Rebekah into her mother’s world. Rebekah begins to prove herself as a reporter as she learns more and more about the case and the ultra-Orthodox Jewish religion.

I like the subject matter of this book and learned things I never knew about the Hasidic Jewish community. I knew about some of their traditions, but didn’t realize how strong the community’s influence is on local politics in Brooklyn.

The book is suspenseful and I was curious to find out who had committed the murder and would the killer be brought to justice. Rebekah is an interesting main character. She is intelligent, resourceful, and hard-working, but not always likeable. As a reporter, she is kind, compassionate, and loyal to her sources, and is someone you would want on your side, if not necessarily as a friend. However, she is able to get to the bottom of this tragic crime and the book’s ending resolves the case, but sets up a situation for what sounds like an intriguing follow-up story if there is a sequel.

title: The Victory Garden: A Novel
text:
A lovely story about love and loss and friendship. A quick and satisfying read. Grab a cup of tea and get cozy.
.

title: Almost Dead (Lizzy Gardner Book 5)
text: I have been  following  Lizzie since her abduction. Love Lizzie but her sidekicks can be a little bizarre.

title: Cute Cats Activity Book for Kids: 70 Activities Including Coloring, Dot-to-Dots & Spot the Difference
text: My niece enjoys this book even if she can't do all the activities quite yet. She and her brother really like all the cat pictures.

title: The Woman in the Window: A Novel
text:
Anna Fox worked as a child psychologist but now spends her days inside her $3,000,000 Manhattan brownstone, afraid to go outside. She watches movies, visits internet chat rooms for agoraphobics, over medicates, drinks too much and she spies on her new neighbors in the building across the street. Anna was once married to Ed and together had a daughter Olivia, but now she lives alone. One day she thinks she's witnessed a horrible crime, but then she is an unreliable narrator, so did something awful really happen or did she imagine it?

This book started out kind of slow for me, but gradually the tension and excitement builds. I figured out part some of the twists but not all. Overall, an enjoyable read that made me want to watch lots of old movies once again.

Rating 4/5 stars

title: No Way Back: A Novel
text:
Suburban wife and mother Wendy Gould is in the wrong place at the wrong time and now her life will never be the same. She is lucky to survive, but her life has become a nightmare. Dedicated nanny Lauritzia Velez also finds herself in a dangerous situation beyond her control. Can Wendy and Lauritzia survive against ruthless drug lords and corrupt government officials or will the violence and corruption be too much for them to overcome?

"No Way Back" is an exciting thriller that shows two ordinary women struggling to put horrendous events behind them and find the strength to fight for their own lives. The book is full of "edge of your seat" suspense that leaves you eager to find out what will happen next. The book would have been even better if I had like Wendy more. I felt sorry for her and grew to respect her determination, but some of the poor decisions she makes and the way she often turns a bad situation into something worse, made it hard for me to like her. On the other hand, I immediately connected with Lauritzia and grew to appreciate her strength even more as the story progressed. I also admired Lauritzia's employers Roxanne and Harold Bachman for their willingness to do what's right and help Lauritzia, even when it put their own lives in danger. The bond between Lauritzia and the Bachmans is heartwarming and one of the best parts of the book.

This is a fast-paced, but serious book. There are many scary and intense scenes that are sometimes difficult to read. I was surprised at the way the story of Lauritzia and Wendy was tied in with the prologue which describes three college students on a fun Spring Break trip when tragedy strikes. However, for a book filled with violence, the ending is surprisingly full of hope. I enjoyed the book and think readers who have liked other solo books by Andrew Gross or who enjoy thrillers by Harlan Coben or Linwood Barclay will like the suspense of "No Way Back".

title: Love, Dishonor, Marry, Die, Cherish, Perish: A Novel
text: David Rakoff presented us with a poetry and prose hybrid that conveys lives intertwined throughout decades.  Unfortunately,  my copy of [[ASIN:038553521X Love, Dishonor, Marry, Die, Cherish, Perish: A Novel]] is a bound galley which lacks the artwork that's included in the final publication, so I haven't yet seen the images as illustrated by Chip Kidd and Seth.  Despite the placemarked artwork pages, this novel wowed me beyond words.  I don't get how such an incredible talent could have published this final installment and then shortly following, taken his last breath.  I don't believe a better example of American studies has been published recently and I'm not familiar with another that's presented in verse.  As I read, I found myself speaking the passages aloud as if I were reading to a child on the verge of sleep.  Whether or not one understands the messages, the cadence and chuckles which often followed the thoughts would likely be soothing.  Rakoff has created in a very brief novel lives rich with emotions and experiences which must be read more than once by this reader.  This novel had more of an impact on me than any other I've read in the past year.  At least.

title: Infinite Powers: How Calculus Reveals the Secrets of the Universe
text:
When I was in college I majored in mathematics, and calculus wasn’t as big a challenge to me as it is for others (statistics, however, is another matter entirely). Calculus was the backbone of my major and it was foundational when it came to most of the other courses I took, and this included advanced algebra and more abstract mathematics. Now, that said, in many cases I understood the “how” but not the “why”. In a few cases, I saw some parallels with algebra and how calculus could short-cut certain processes (sort of) to arrive at the same result, but even so, the subject remained a black art even though it was clear [to me] what to do.

What this book does is explain some of the why. It’s not a dry, dull read that throws theorems and formulas at you with brief explanations. Instead, there are real-world examples that show why calculus is the means to the end. Instead of gagging you with straight theory and practice like a class textbook, it brings calculus down to a level that makes it much more interesting and, in a tangible way, fun. Now, it does not offer comprehensive coverage, of course; my calculus textbook from college is a full 1,000 pages and covers a lot of more obscure stuff that this book doesn’t mention, but that’s not this book’s intention. The idea here is to give a more high-level coverage with application.

I sure wish I had this book when I was struggling with the subject; knowing the “why” makes the “how” a lot easier to work through.

I’m still working my way through this book, but so far it has been very enjoyable and thought provoking. What I have read so far has made the subject of calculus a lot more interesting, even though I already have a handle on the mechanics of it. If you have struggled with calculus, this book is a way to build more understanding and appreciation. If you’re more curious and just want to know what it’s about, it’s a good starting point.

title: The Ultimate Puzzle Book for Kids: Sudokus, Mazes, Crosswords, and More!
text:
I love that this puzzle book is made from thick, quality paper that makes for smoothing writing and erasing. I also love the fact that the pages are so colorful. Some puzzle books have great puzzles but the presentation is lacking and/or the paper is hard to write on. The colors and the variety of different types of puzzles will help keep kids from getting bored after a few puzzles. I myself prefer puzzle books that have a variety instead of all one type of puzzle.

The book is organized into four sections - Easy, Medium, Hard, and Games for Two. There is also an answer key at the end. The book has both number puzzles (such as Sudoku and Futoshiki), word puzzles such as crosswords and word searches, as well as mazes, logic puzzles, and others. The games for two include tic tac toe, hangman, dots, word jumble, and a couple of other popular games.

The book is geared towards kids between nine and twelve years old. However, as an adult I enjoyed several of the harder puzzles. My favorites were the Futoshiki and the logic puzzles. I actually thought a few of the puzzles in the easier chapters were a little tricky, especially for a nine-year-old. Others such as the word searches and “Find The Differences” are fun for all ages. The logic problems in the Easy section are the perfect introduction to kids on how to solve this type of puzzle. This would be a good book that siblings of different ages could share.

Although I love the bright colors throughout the book, there is a downside. On some of the pages, the places where the answers are supposed to be written are too dark of a shade of blue or green and it’s hard to see what you’ve written there, especially with a pencil. You can write the answers off to the side or below where it’s indicated, but this could have been better if the colors in these instances were a lighter shade.

One other thing that could be better is the answer key. The font is so small on some of the solutions, especially the word searches, that it does no good. A few more pages devoted to the answer section allowing each solution to be printed in a larger font would have made this so much better.

title: The Last Train to Zona Verde: My Ultimate African Safari
text:
I have not read Paul Theroux in years, but in my twenties, I drank his travel books in. Now we are both older, maybe wiser, but also looking at a different world. Theroux, now in his 70's is obviously reprioritizing his time and risks.

This books reads like an abortive journey into Conrad's Heart of Darkness. But an alternate version where Marlow turns back before he confronted Kurtz face to face. As Theroux works his way up the west coast of Africa he descends deeper and deeper into the poverty, coruption and crime that is today's reality of Africa. SPOILER ALERT: His original goal was to reach Timbuktu, but looking north from turbulent and Corrupt Angola, he sees a patchwork of military coups, Islamist Terrorist groups and states and societies in such a failed state to make going on foolhardy if not suicidal. So unlike Marlow, he realizes he's seen enough, he no longer has a need to pursue what would be more of a macho stunt than a educational holiday. But his progress is enough to draw you through the book into darker and stormier lands.

Theroux's observation and analysis is still honed to a frank and cutting sharpness. He cuts through myths and perceptions and it feels that he is taking you along, side by side. And he takes you through an amazing back alley tour of Southwestern Africa.

I found this to be an amazing book. As a kid I used to get a strange new magazine, strange in that it covered the Angolan and Mozambique wars weekly. Angola has been off the radar during the next three decades, so it was great to get an insider view of the country. And it was a deeply informative look, unapologetically showing the effects of decades of civil war, centuries of colonization, and the inequalities of Aid and Oil profit dispersement. Angola is an amazing story that is told far too little.

All in all another great book from an aging master. It is dark but not completely fatalistic. Theroux lays out what lies in the future. As Africans move to cities, shanties will grow, Shanty towns will become towns, natural resources will be stretched, poverty will grow, a reaction will happen. But things will go on. And there is a counter balancing buoyancy every time a person steps up to do the right thing. As dark as things appear, there is an individual level where goodness lies.

I'm better for having read this book.

title: The Leader's Code: Mission, Character, Service, and Getting the Job Done
text:
The author, a former Captain in the US Marines, defines what their servant leadership model is all about and how it applies to every one of us. He believes our society is suffering from a leadership crisis.

Great leadership starts with a mission that includes service to something or someone beyond our personal welfare, has clear measurable goals, and the highest of ethical standards. In order to carry out that mission, one must possess:

- humility (maintaining a realistic and consistent view of self and relationships)
- excellence (putting out our best efforts)
- kindness (operating from a place of love)
- discipline (ensuring shared acceptance of common beliefs on right and wrong and adhering to or executing the same rules for self and others)
- courage (risking our lives or livelihoods for others)
- wisdom (maintaining soundness of judgement relative to the situation presented; this is not the same as intelligence)

I've simpilfied his description of the virtues above. The author goes into a lot of depth and provides excellent examples of each characteristic. For instance, the author describes the death of 100 men on the Thetis (British submarine). These 100 men died 100 feet down from the rescue boat simply because the rescuers believed whatever conclusion was most favorable instead of seeing that the Davis apparatus that was rescuing the crew was actually not working as anticipated. In this case, the rescuers did not exhibit wisdom.

Although the author says great leadership cannot happen without sacrifice, I prefer to think of a sacrifice as making a choice to do one action over another. Besides the virtues described above, he brings into focus achieving balance in life, setting measurable goals, evaluating the energy and money expended, etc. For instance, we need to expend the right energy in the right places. If we are "physically alongside our children but mentally distant while we check our email on our cell phones" then we are not really engaged in the activity we say we are (spending time with our family).

To me this book is excellent and contains a lot of great examples which helps explain each virtue. However, since the servant leadership code is based on the military, I think many people will find this code hard to embrace. I do feel following these principles makes great leaders but I also feel not many people can easily follow this code.

title: My Name Is Lucy Barton: A Novel
text:
I have loved Elizabeth Strout's writing ever since I read--and reread--"Olive Kitteridge," so I leapt at the chance to review her new book, "My Name is Lucy Barton." This latest of her books is shorter than the previous two, and I think it is more personal as well, though I have no way of knowing how much of the action, if any, comes from real life.

As the book opens, Lucy Barton, a successful writer, is in the hospital with a mysterious complication of surgery that is never fully explained. She remains in the hospital for nine weeks, largely isolated from the outside world--her husband hates hospitals and rarely visits, and her two daughters are too young to come on their own. Suddenly, Lucy's mother, whom she has not seen in several years, shows up and stays with her in the hospital for five days. During this time, the action alternates between the present and Lucy's past, her childhood and young adulthood, and the reader gradually comes to an understanding of both Lucy and her mother. The two reminisce about various events and persons from the past, and in particular, they discuss the marriages of various friends and relatives. As the book develops, the reader sees that all this talk of other people's marriages is a way for both Lucy and her mother to avoid the elephant in the room, their own marriages and relationships.

Lucy's mother's visit is not all sunshine and roses. In chapters alternating between past and present, we see the truly terrifying past that Lucy has overcome, and her mother's limited awareness of the damage she has done to her daughters and son. Lucy's brother and sister are mentioned in small but important glimpses into the past, and the reader sees the childhood of poverty and abuse that is her heritage. Despite all this, however, Lucy loves her mother and desperately wants her approval. She slowly comes to a realization of who she is--the time in the hospital could be compared to an extended retreat, without the religious element--and we see the results of her epiphany in the last part of the book.

I loved the way this book was written. There is very little action; most of what happens is conversation and memory and conversation about memory. The point of the book is the development of a self, an identity, an awareness of who you really are--thus the title: "My Name is Lucy Barton." The book reads very quickly--it has fewer than 200 pages--but it will stay with you long after you have read it. I recommend this book for anyone who enjoys contemporary literary women's fiction.

title: Blood Rites (The Dresden Files, Book 6)
text:
Harry Dresden, the only wizard/private detective listed in the Chicago yellow pages runs into some family problems in this, the sixth novel in the Dresden Files series. Rather ironic since Dresden is an orphan and his relationship with his girlfriend Susan has hit a serious snag - her being halfway turned into a vampire has caused a few problems. Still Harry has had to keep on taking on jobs in order to pay the rent. As the story opens Harry has rescued a litter of puppies from the demons who had kidnapped them. The mission seemed to have reached a satisfactory conclusion when he returned the pups, even if one had been inadvertently left behind in Harry's car. After all it shouldn't be all that difficult to send it on its way as soon as he got a minute to spare right? What Harry didn't realize was that the simple body guarding assignment for a film director would lead him in the path of the dysfunctional ruling family of the White Court vampires. Harry had met part of the White Court earlier, the vampire Thomas had aided Harry in the past for reasons that Harry could not quite understand. As if that was not enough Harry's lone ally on the CPD, Det. Murphy had her own family problems that spilled over into Harry's life. Harry's mentor, Ebenezar, the closest known thing Harry had to family even arrived on the scene to lend a hand. Before all questions were answered though Harry's world would change in ways that he never imagined possible.

The DRESDEN FILES are a popular series of urban dark fantasies set in Chicago and featuring Wizard Harry Dresden and his efforts to keep the magical world of the Nevernever from spilling into the mundane world. The overall story arc in this series is quite pronounced so for maximum enjoyment begin reading this at the beginning, STORM FRONT and proceed in order.

title: Eat Feel Fresh: A Contemporary, Plant-Based Ayurvedic Cookbook
text:
This is a gorgeous book! I don't follow an Ayurvedic diet, but I am a vegetarian. I also try to stay away from sugar and unhealthy carbs. (Healthy carbs are welcome in my day and are in this cookbook.) I desperately want to eat less dairy, too. I don't overly like to cook, though, so I need super simple recipes. Eating is a constant stress for me and it can get too complicated. I greatly appreciate that I can eat everything within this book. (I showed my husband this cookbook and told him that if he ever wanted to cook for me, every recipe was an option.) The recipes are mostly simple and I already have many of the ingredients used within my pantry. (That said, I do already have many "specialty" ingredients one uses when trying to stay away from sugar and unhealthy carbs.)

This is a great cookbook to try if you're wanting to eat more plant based meals, even if you're not attempting an Ayurvedic way of eating. I adore it!

title: Hippie Animals Coloring Book (Coloring is Fun) (Design Originals) 32 Groovy, Totally Chill Animal Designs from Thaneeya McArdle, on High-Quality, Extra-Thick Perforated Pages Resist Bleed-Through

title: Clean Eating Kitchen: The Low-Carb Mediterranean Cookbook: Quick and Easy High-Protein, Low-Sugar, Healthy-Fat Recipes for Lifelong Health-More Than ... Meals to Prepare in 30 Minutes or Less
text: It always feels like KETO-minded cookbooks focus so much on the meats and cheeses with little veggies plus all the desserts. It's nice to see a truly healthy version of KETO that includes legumes and plant protein. Yes, you CAN eat these, smaller amounts and with other foods, and still be in ketosis. My family is from Italy, and I find I thrive on keto and Mediterranean style eating so this book truly is perfect for my needs!

title: Rosetta, Rosetta, Sit by Me!
text:
A perfect book to introduce to our young about the hurt and harm bigotry can do.

Based on the life of Rosetta Douglass, daughter abolitionist Frederick Douglass.

In 1848 (12 years before the Civil War) it's 9 year old Rosetta's first day at a exclusively white girl school in Rochester, NY. The students are excited to have her in their class and she is excited to be there and learn. The teacher, who is a bigot, isolates Rosetta until the father she adores involves the principal and begins a mission to desegregate the school district. Rosetta is told to leave the school.

Rosetta goes to school in Albany, NY and her father works for equal schooling, finally Rosetta returns home from boarding school and becomes a school teacher herself.

The illustrator renders beautiful charcoal artwork, however Rosetta's bandanna wearing mother didn't match her social standing. So I researched it and it is derived from historical accounts at the time on file at the Rochester Public Library. In a letter from Minnie Bishop in which she wrote that Anna would wear a "dark cotton dress and a red bandanna on her head."

For the history geek in me extras at the end of the book were terrific and gave a completed picture of the lives of Rosetta and Mr. Douglass.

title: Find Me (Inland Empire Book 1)
text:
Wow! A real page turner. Story is filled with dark family secrets and edge of your seat twists and turns.

Reni Fischer is a young woman and a former FBI profiler agent. She decided to leave the agency because of flash backs from her childhood. Reni ‘s father , Benjamin Fisher , was a serial killer. He used his innocent very young daughter to lure his victims and then kill them.
Thirty years later, Benjamin is on death row. Daniel Ellis who isa San Bernadino detective has been studying Benjamin’ case histories and trying to find all the dead bodies who were victims. Benjamin contacts Daniel and wants to make a deal with him. If he brings Reni to see him and gets a one day pass to go back to the desert, he will tell Daniel where all the bodies of his victims are. Daniel contacts Reni and convinces her to see her father after 30 years of no contact. This opens up a pandora,s box of dark family secrets and
actions.

Miss Frazier is a gifted writer. The pictures she paints of the Mojave desert and life in the desert as well as great research and believable attributes of her characters minds and actions keep the reader totally engaged.

Can’t wait to see Book two of this series. I definitely recommend this book.

Favorite quote. There was no starting over for most people. That was a misconception. Unless a person’s memory could be erased, there were no fresh starts, only progression.

title: Robert's Rules of Order Newly Revised
text:
While there certainly are other options to understanding parliamentary procedure, "Robert's Rules of Order" is a traditional key to knowing the ways things are done.

True, true, this isn't exactly the sort of book you bring to a coffeehouse on a Saturday. However, it is also true that if you are part of an organization that has organized meetings, 'Robert's Rules' is a great place to start. The liturgy of meeting procedure starts and finishes with the rules set forth in 'Robert's Rules.'

Get this book, but consider getting one of the plain English versions as well. If you are new to parliamentary procedure, you'll find both books in tandem quite helpful.

I fully recommend, "Robert's Rules of Order" by Henry M. Robert III.

Anthony Trendl
americanspeechwriter.com

title: Ready Player One: A Novel

title: Fallout: The Vault Dweller's Official Cookbook
text: So I love the game Fallout and I introduced my daughter to it. Turns out she loves it too so we decided to get this cookbook just for fun.  Oh my goodness, the recipes are legitimate gorme recipes. We have tried 3 of them so far and they've been spectacular. I can't believe the amazing twist the recipes provide and how absolutely delicious they turn out. It may be geared towards video game fans but this recipe book is no joke. I'm wondering how I got through all these years without it.

title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar
text: This is a must have in any child's library.  Beautiful today as it was decades ago.

title: Dragons Coloring Book (Dover Fantasy Coloring Books)
text: Great quality paper and a large selection of different dragons to choose to color.  My 7 year old son and I have 4 of these books.  I initially bought one to try and learn how to shade and define scales and wings and my very active 7 year old asks me to color again and again with these books.  I'm particularly fond of the quality of the paper and the helpful dots to learn how to shade.  We have prismacolor, CarbOthello and Derwent colored pencils to choose from.  The quality of the paper allows us to saturate colors.  For the price, this book can't be beat.

title: My First Superman Book: Touch and Feel (DC Super Heroes)

text: do not like the narrators way of reading the book, overall very intriguing and interesting

title: Watercolor With Me in the Forest
text: I am new to painting, in general. Basics are covered, wet on dry, wet on wet, fur, ink and wash. I've completed the first few pages and feel confident, I can finish. It's like a single subject, watercoloring book as images are provided. Paper does make a difference. Drawings are simple enough to create on desired paper. But, as I paid for the book, I'll gladly use what's given. This book is an easier guide than many other books on the subject that I've purchased. I recommend it for motivated newcomers. I don't seem to have all the colors, but I'll use what I have, i.e.  all prima marketing sets, a koi field set, an $8 set from Michael's. I find that Grumbacher brushes are excellent.  I have a tiny detail brush as well. To transfer image to paper of choice, make a copy, trace from back with pencil. Then, place on new paper, and go over lines with a pen. The graphite will transfer the image. Plus, you can have as many do overs as needed.

title: Microsoft Sculpt Comfort Mouse - Black. Comfortable design, Customizable Windows Touch Tab, 4-Way Scrolling,Bluetooth Mouse for PC/Laptop/Desktop, works with Mac/Windows Computers
text:
*update*
after using this mouse for more than a month, I started to have pain in my thumb - I switch out mice and eventually the pain went away -there is an awkward groove that your thumb has to sit in. now I am sorry i got these

Pros:
This connects up right away with my my mac book pro, it has decent accuracy even on surfaces that are less than ideal. Much more reasonable price that most device of this type. Really decent battery life.
Scroll wheel is a little tight, but responsive and has both left and right click buttons. Has a manual off switch to save battery life. Connects right back automatically after turning off (to one device - see below)

Cons
It does frequently disconnect even during use, but will come back if you wiggle it. It is a little annoying if you are working on documents but if you were playing a game or giving a presentation this would be more problematic.
I can't get it to un-connect from one laptop and connect to another device - not another laptop or desktop. That is only a problem if you want to switch it out between devices, but if you want that you should get something else because it wont work.

Summary - for my needs it was ok and worth the price.

Update

have had a couple of these now (hard to find a decently priced mouse without a dongle) and they all quit working after a couple months. The replacements also had the same problem. Either some click functions don't work, or the scroll wheel, or both . Gave up on this brand and am happy with the Logitech Bluetooth Mouse M557 for PC, Mac and Windows 8 Tablets (910-0 03971) https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00JUHDYSO/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1 at a very good price

title: Logitech Ultrathin Keyboard Cover for iPad 2 and New iPad
text:
I purchased my keyboard on June 15, 2012 and have had it 3 months. At first I loved it. I had the older Logitech case before this one and gave the older one to my mom. I liked the feel of the keys better and really liked the magnetic strip that held the Ipad in place.

But - after a few weeks of use (6-8 hours a day) the following started:

1.) Shortly after purchase the white strip where the Ipad sits started to come unglued. As other reviewers mentioned this led to sticky line/spots on my Ipad screen. It also causes a lot of dirt and gunk to get stuck.
2.) Finally the little strip basically moved up and out of the slot - rendering it useless - but still sticky.
3.) In the last few days the worst thing has happened. The strip is no longer magnetic. I don't know how this could happen but there no hold at all when I put my IPad in the slot. This has caused my Ipad to just fall off the keyboard on many occassions - even if it's just sitting on a table and not being used. Worse - it has caused me to drop my Ipad three times. Thank goodness each time was on a padded floor and I didn't damage the Ipad itself.

I purchased mine at Best Buy and am going to try to return or exchange it this week.

I can't recommend this keyboard as it is made now. Hopefully Logitech will resolve this issue.

title: NETGEAR Nighthawk Smart WiFi Router (R7000P) - AC2300 Wireless Speed (up to 2300 Mbps) | Up to 2000 sq ft Coverage & 35 Devices | 4 x 1G Ethernet and 2 USB ports | Armor Security
text:
I bought this router to replace an old Actiontec that was no longer keeping up too well. I'm over six months in at this point and while this has been a very slim upgrade over the older router, it's caused me far too many headaches. This is not a quality product.

Here is my tale, in detail:

Router setup was fairly simple, although I was unable to get the various wizards and configurators to do what I wanted. You have to actively fight with this router in order to not have it force you to do its "smart" setup, which is annoying. But eventually I managed to get it working, enabling both 2.4GHz and 5GHz networks.

At first, everything was great and super fast and then... (You knew it was coming...) 5GHz started to cut out, and eventually disappeared. Why? Who knows. Go search the Netgear forums, as I've spent many hours doing, and you'll find countless reports of the same.

And then... (Yes, there's more!) One day my router randomly bricked itself for no apparent reason. So I called support and had probably the worst product support experience of my life. This product, as it turns out, is not even supposed to be supported past 90 days. After opening no fewer than five cases in order to argue, I finally got Netgear to agree to send me a replacement. (We're talking several hours of work on my part to get that done, and lots of nastiness on behalf of the "customer service" people on Netgear's side.)

Now I've had the replacement running for a couple of months. 5GHz is still dead. 2.4GHz works, but every couple of weeks performance suddenly grinds to a halt and I have to reset the router. Latency is all over the place, and even when it's "good" throughput is still bad -- I can get at most 30Mb/s down, wireless. (Tested five feet away from the router.)

And today, yet another problem has arisen: My "connected devices" list is now not listing all of the connected devices. Back to the Netgear forums, and yet again there are loads of threads on this topic. And on the subject of connected devices, this router also lacks decent configuration options for limiting specific devices on the network. Perhaps that's buried in the "Armor" product, which is a paid add-on that the router tries to pitch at you every time you log in -- but I'm certainly not going to upgrade to find out.

So there you have it. The hardware seems decent, but the software is garbage and the support is nonexistent. I'm eyeing an alternative firmware at this point (DD-WRT or similar), in hopes that I can at least get something from this router.

I highly, highly recommend avoiding this product unless you enjoy wasting valuable time and having your connection speed occasionally drop for no good reason. A router should be a commodity item that "just works" and keeps working, and this one simply doesn't fit the bill.

title: REOLINK Security Camera Outdoor, Wireless Rechargable Battery Powered, 1080P Starlight Night Vision, PIR Motion Video for Home Surveillance (1 Pack)
text:
The camera quality is fine and live viewing through the app is fine (Android version anyway). What is TERRIBLE: Security camera features. Bottom of the barrel. There is a sensitivity to motion slider for when the camera starts, but that is ALL. No way of mapping out areas you want to ignore (e.g sidewalk or pets). "Free" cloud is a rippoff. You get 8 SECOND highly compressed clips!! That's it. If you store locally on an SD card you can get up to 30 seconds.

If you want this camera for security monitoring, unless 8 second snippets work for you, look elsewhere. You can't even pay them to get longer recordings. Since I bought this thing because of the free cloud service, I consider this a total ripoff. Dishonest.

Through the windows app: The settings are confusing. The interface is awful. You can't set it to automatically open a live view. Instead you have to open & click on an icon. You can't leave it in live view (it will automatically shut off after a little while "to save battery." Isn't that my choice? I should be able to use the battery if I want to. I like to leave the live view open when I'm expecting someone.

I would not buy this again. Look to the real brands like linksys, Nest, Ring, etc. if you actually want a security camera and not a little toy.

title: Screen Protector Cable Data Nylon USB AL 05
text:
Amazon 1 Star Rating equals to "I Hate It" ... and I do have hatred for aspects of the Bolt, even though I also have love for it.

(Please keep in mind I have owned many different DVR's, such as the Moxi, ReplayTV, and Channel Master DVR+.)

I Hate:

TiVo wants a fee of $600 for a lifetime license, after you pay $300 or $400 for your Bolt. This is insanity! I could understand if they were asking for $600 for the lifetime of the person, no matter what TiVo boxes they swap out in the future.

The design of the box is terrible, in my opinion. I really hate it. (And please do not say they did it only to allow air to escape, because air can escape just fine on their Roamios and on other DVR's such as the Moxi.)

Final thing I hate is the difficulty prying the box apart to swap out the hard drive after the screws are removed. TiVo knows one's hard drive will fail eventually after the warranty, and/or that people will wanna upgrade to a larger capacity drive. They could have easily designed this box with a removable cover just under the drive, instead of making it where you have to take the whole cover off in parts.

Love:

Hands down, the TiVo interface and extra features of the Bolt are superior to any other DVR out there as of this writing. Once you have the opportunity to use Commercial Skip, you'll fall in love. Also, the Quick Mode feature is very nice, and it works well. The speed of the Bolt interface is excellent.

The TiVo software is really easy to use. The Bolt is easy to get set up. I like that if you have to swap out your hard drive, the TiVo software will automatically install itself on the bare new drive.

I like the online and mobile interfaces which allow you to set recordings remotely.

There are countless people online who feel that TiVo's $15 per month (or $150 per year) fee is too expensive ... and of course if you are paying it, you will wish the fee was a more reasonable $10 per month/$100 per year.

I am hoping TiVo has heard the endless complaints of folks who hate the Bolt's box design and who want a flat box. It will be interesting to see if they do a redesign of their next DVR model. And I'm hoping they come out with a TiVo Bolt OTA for cable cutters where the lifetime license fee will be far more reasonably priced than the Bolt's astronomical $600.

title: APC UPS Battery Backup for Computer, BE550G Surge Protector with Battery Backup, Dataline Protection
text: The first time the power went out, this thing worked like a champ, and kept all my networking equipment running perfectly. However, when the power came back on, the unit did not do what I expected: Instead of reverting back to drawing A/C power, it continued to use the battery. Finally, after some time, it started beeping to indicate that the battery was low. I double checked the electrical outlet, and power was good. I unplugged the unit, and plugged it back in but it still did not recognize that grid power was available. Eventually the battery ran down all the way, and it shut down all my equipment. Surely this cannot be how it's intended to work!

Another advantage of a microfiber cleaning cloth is that it's washable.

Because of the rounded corners, the Targus pads are useless for cleaning a tablet's square corners, if the tablet is enclosed in a protective case. Maybe the pads would clean the corners if I pressed hard, but I'm not willing to risk breaking the touchscreen! I rate this product at 2 stars ("I don't like it" on the Amazon scale).

title: SanDisk Sansa m250 2 GB MP3 Player (Black)
text:
Sandisk is discontinuing this model, thus the 80% price break. While that's not necessarily a mark against the unit, the software glitches I've experienced from Day 1 certainly are. A quick check of the Sandisk discussion boards reveals that a number of people (including myself) have experienced freezing of the unit right after a file transfer. This can be fixed by plugging in the drive to the USB port with the "hold" button turned on (ensuring MSC mode, which makes the unit behave like a flash drive) and formatting the drive with an FAT32 format. Why, after multiple firmware upgrades for this model, this is necessary to do so is beyond me. That procedure does seems to have fixed it for the moment. I am disappointed to note that there is no way to play all of the files loaded onto the unit as a group unless you create a playlist and transfer your files with MediaMonkey or Windows Media Player. If you use straight drag and drop to transfer your mp3s as a folder to the unit, the player will show you each artist, album, or song individually but has no provisions to play all of the music arranged in a particular folder. Also, be aware that the photograph above is quit misleading. The angle obscures the rounded back of the unit and its much thicker than you think (about an inch at its thickest part). I bought this unit to replace my beloved but limited-capacity Creative MuVo. Those are also being discontinued and the 2 GB version is coming down in price. If you have the opportunity, check out that one instead.

Sept '08 Update: Stay far far away from this one, guys. I tried giving this as a gift and I've been feeling bad about it ever since. It just doesn't work very well at all. The software freezes more often than not and it's so much work just to load it with songs that you'll find yourself just doing without most of the time. Get something by Creative instead, even if you pay a little more. Those people know how to make mp3 players easy to use!

title: D-Link WiFi Range Extender, AC1750 Plug In Wall Booster, Dual Band Gigabit Wireless Repeater and Smart Signal Indicator (DAP-1720),White
text: Initial unpacking and setup weren't terribly difficult. I'm not the most electronics-savvy person in the world, but I do know how to program a DVR ;-) After carefully following setup instructions, I got the extender connected. All seemed to be working fine. My preset router is upstairs in one end of the house and I put the extender downstairs fairly centrally located in the house. I had all three bars lit up and the antenna all extended. Man, this thing looks like it could transmit to Mars. I really wanted it to work, although reception with my present router throughout the house isn't really that much of a problem. But I love electronics and saw it as just a little "insurance" and added reception when out on the patio. Well, on both the 2.4 and 5 ghz, I had problems. I use an iPad and iPhone a lot on WiFi, as well as a laptop. Now and then, all would work fine. But most of the time, I had a lot of trouble getting webpages to load. I'd go back to operating directly from the router, all fine. Back to the extender, slow loading. I've given it several days of operation to see change, but suffice it to say, when I'm operating from the extender, I have trouble. When from the router, no trouble. I can't imagine what I'm missing, but it's just not working for me. I'll give it two stars, simply giving benefit of the doubt, but I believe I'm doing everything right.

title: Amazon Basics Wireless Remote Control for Specific Canon Digital SLR Cameras, Black, 0.28" x 1.10" x 3.36"
text: It's definitely an economical option, but it works to its value. It's easy to use, requiring no set up or anything, but it's working range is very narrow and therefore inconvenient and difficult to use. It does work better in shorter range, but I still had to to aim up and down to find the spot. title: ULAK iPod Touch 7 Case,iPod Touch 6 & 5 Case, Clear Slim Hybrid Bumper TPU/Scratch Resistant Hard PC Back/Corner Shock Absorption Case for Apple iPod Touch 5th 6th 7th Generation, Black

title: JETech Screen Protector for iPad 2 3 4 (Oldest Models), Tempered Glass Film, 1-Pack
text: After reading reviews I thought I'd try this protector. Application was easy enough but I have about 8 air bubbles from dime sized to quarter sized that won't smooth out no matter how much I rub or smooth out. Irritating to say the least.

title: RCA Galileo 11.5" 32 GB Touchscreen Tablet Computer with Keyboard Case Quad-Core 1.3Ghz Processor 1GB Memory 32GB HDD Webcam Wifi Bluetooth Android 6.0 - Charcoal
text:
I really wanted to like this. The bargain price was incredible for an 11.5" tablet with detachable keyboard. And a lot of really good reviews. And I was getting it as a second, backup device, not my primary, so it didn't need to be great, it just needed to do the job well enough. It isn't. It's pitiful.

Battery life: 1-2 hrs, and the high alerts go off at 20%, and below that you're supposed to shut of the computer while it charges back to 20.

Keyboard: some reviewers actually praised it. The keys have good travel, which is helpful, but they are so small I can't touch-type (notice how there is 1" of unused space next to keyboard -- so don't compare it to other 11.5" laptops you may have used). Maybe if you have tiny hands it will work better, but even then, I don't like the layout. E.g., the shift key on the right is small -- good luck ever hitting that without looking down.

Touchpad: no acceleration, so it's always going fast. No way to adjust it. End-result: very difficult to make fine movements. But very easy to accidentally touch it while typing, thus tossing the cursor to a different place in your writing.

Touch screen: don't know if it's the processor being slow or the touch screen having poor sensitivity, but I often have to repeatedly touch screen to get it to do something.

I don't like writing negative reviews. I know how hard it is to create good things and sell them. But I can't believe that even RCA thinks this is even adequate. Seems they took the cheapest of every component they could find and put it together in a box for sale.

Returning it...

title: Avantree Audition Pro 40 hr Bluetooth Over Ear Headphones with Mic for Music, Calls & TV, APTX Low Latency, Multipoint, Foldable Headset, Wireless & Wired for PC Computer Phone

title: F FORITO 2-Pack 13.3 inch Anti Blue Light Laptop Screen Protector, Anti Glare & Anti Scratch Screen Filter Compatible with 13” Lenovo/ASUS/Dell/HP/Acer/HP/SAMSUNG with 16:9 Aspect Ratio

title: SAMSUNG Galaxy Watch Active (40MM, GPS, Bluetooth ) Smart Watch with Fitness Tracking, and Sleep Analysis - Black  (US Version)
text:
Update: 8/21/2020- so the battery has been draining VERY quickly the last few weeks. It charges to 100% but then by 4 hours it's at a 43% charges with it just being on my wrist with nothing really running. I've reset the watch, made sure it has the latest updates and turned off a lot of the necessary bloat but no luck. Not sure what to do with it at this time.

6/21/2020: Arrived without a scratch on the watch or band. Charger and extra watch band was also included. Definitely like new. I was a little worried as the packaging was a little grimy. I do have a skinny wrist and I've had a 42mm smartwatch prior (which to me was the perfect size) so putting on a 40mm made it seem "small" even on my skinny wrist. But I found that adding a watch cover "bulked it" up just enough for me so I'm happy!
I cannot stand any of the Samsung related health apps. Honestly I'm only using this watch for running so Im only using the UA app and cell phone-less usage of music. A note but you need to disconnect the phone from the watch so that the watch is operating on its own IF you are wanting to use the watch for music. I run without my phone and didn't realize that if you don't disconnect the watch from the phone (you can do this via the app on the phone), the watch thinks it's still tethered to the phone and the music will play to the extent that the connection to the phone lasts.

Overall a nice looking watch and I'll see how the renewed product works out...more updates later!!

title: Logitech Performance MX Wireless Mouse (DISCONTINUED and Replaced by MX Master 2S)

title: Garmin Portable Friction Mount - Frustration Free Packaging
text: Sadly this did not work in our truck or with our Garmin Diezel GPS.  It vibrated off the dash constantly.  The attachment did not fit the GPS properly and was too loose.  You couldn't adjust the GPS to face you at a particular angle.  It was too loose and just flopped downward. Maybe it was just a fluke. title: FLAGTOP Gaming Laptop Fan Cooling Pad, 3 Modes of Fans Switcher, 2400RPM, Portable Laptop Cooling Fan with 2 USB Ports, 5 Fans, Quiet, Slim, Blue LED (Cord Not Included, male USB A to male USB A)

My old five fan cooling pad burned out after a few years and I thought this would be an excellent replacement with the same cooling power as my original one. however, they are not all the same and this one does not have half the cooling power of my single fan pad, at the highest speed.
